segment.source: ToolbarsExplorer
segment.target: ToolbarsExplorer

segment.source: Author: Alberto Buffolino
segment.target: Author: Alberto Buffolino

segment.source: This add-on makes easier to use toolbars in applications, providing an exploration model derived by object navigation, with simplified gestures.
segment.target: This add-on makes easier to use toolbars in applications, providing an exploration model derived by object navigation, with simplified gestures.

segment.source: Commands
segment.target: Commands

segment.source: Alt+applications: starts toolbars exploration
segment.target: Alt+applications: starts toolbars exploration

segment.source: (you can remap it via NVDA gesture manager, under Object navigation).
segment.target: (you can remap it via NVDA gesture manager, under Object navigation).

segment.source: During exploration, following gestures are available:
segment.target: During exploration, following gestures are available:

segment.source: Left/right arrow: moves to previous/next toolbar;
segment.target: Left/right arrow: moves to previous/next toolbar;

segment.source: Up/down arrow: scrolls up/down items in current toolbar;
segment.target: Up/down arrow: scrolls up/down items in current toolbar;

segment.source: Enter: activates toolbar or its item;
segment.target: Enter: activates toolbar or its item;

segment.source: Space: simulates left mouse click on toolbar or its item;
segment.target: Space: simulates left mouse click on toolbar or its item;

segment.source: Applications/shift+F10: simulates right mouse click on toolbar or its item;
segment.target: Applications/shift+F10: simulates right mouse click on toolbar or its item;

segment.source: Escape: exits from exploration.
segment.target: Escape: exits from exploration.

segment.source: Additionally, you can perform actions on toolbars or its items using any gesture provided by NVDA, as exactly as when you move to objects with standard object navigation.
segment.target: Additionally, you can perform actions on toolbars or its items using any gesture provided by NVDA, as exactly as when you move to objects with standard object navigation.

segment.source: Notes
segment.target: Notes

segment.source: Exploration is terminated explicitly pressing escape, and implicitly:
segment.target: Exploration is terminated explicitly pressing escape, and implicitly:

segment.source: performing an action on toolbar or its item (with space, applications/shift+F10, enter);
segment.target: performing an action on toolbar or its item (with space, applications/shift+F10, enter);

segment.source: pressing a gesture that moves out of current toolbar objects (alt, windows, tab, NVDA+F1, object navigation gestures, etc).
segment.target: pressing a gesture that moves out of current toolbar objects (alt, windows, tab, NVDA+F1, object navigation gestures, etc).

segment.source: Other gestures not containing alt, windows or escape (as h, 1, shift, shift+h, control+z) simply does nothing.
segment.target: Other gestures not containing alt, windows or escape (as h, 1, shift, shift+h, control+z) simply does nothing.

segment.source: Suggestions
segment.target: Suggestions

segment.source: The add-on may fail in Mozilla applications the first time after add-on installation/update; please restart NVDA and Mozilla applications to resolve;
segment.target: The add-on may fail in Mozilla applications the first time after add-on installation/update; please restart NVDA and Mozilla applications to resolve;

segment.source: In LibreOffice, best configuration is probably default or single toolbar, set it on view menu/toolbar position.
segment.target: In LibreOffice, best configuration is probably default or single toolbar, set it on view menu/toolbar position.
